name: MADRIGAL PHARMACEUTICALS, INC.
business: MADRIGAL PHARMACEUTICALS, INC. is a biopharmaceutical company focused on delivering novel therapeutics for metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH), a serious liver disease. Its only approved product, Rezdiffra (resmetirom), is a once-daily, oral, liver-directed thyroid hormone receptor beta agonist for the treatment of adults with noncirrhotic MASH with moderate to advanced liver fibrosis. Rezdiffra received accelerated approval from the FDA in March 2024 and conditional marketing authorization from the European Commission in August 2025.

exposures.sentence: Our medication, Rezdiffra (resmetirom), is a once-daily, oral, liver-directed thyroid hormone receptor beta ( THR- ) agonist designed to target key underlying causes of MASH.

Through our business development efforts, we have acquired rights to MGL-2086, an oral glucagon-like peptide-1 ( GLP-1 ) receptor agonist, ervogastat, an oral diacylglycerol O-acyltransferase 2 ( DGAT2 ) inhibitor, six small interfering RNA ( siRNA ) programs and additional preclinical MASH candidates.

catalysts.sentence: We expect results from the OUTCOMES trial in 2027.
We expect outcomes data from this trial in 2028.

catalysts.event: Phase 1 single ascending dose study of MGL-2086
catalysts.sentence: We expect to initiate a single ascending dose study of MGL-2086 in the second quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: Phase 1 drug-to-drug interaction study with ervogastat and resmetirom
catalysts.sentence: In 2026, we expect to conduct a Phase 1 drug-to-drug interaction study with ervogastat and resmetirom.

catalysts.event: Phase 2 combination trial with ervogastat and resmetirom
catalysts.sentence: In addition, we expect to initiate a Phase 2 combination trial with ervogastat and resmetirom in 2027 following discussions with the FDA.

tamClaims.claim: 315,000 patients diagnosed with MASH with moderate to advanced fibrosis under specialist prescribers in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: Based on published epidemiology data and an analysis of medical claims using ICD-10 disease diagnosis codes as of 2023, we estimate that 315,000 patients diagnosed with MASH with moderate to advanced fibrosis (stages F2 to F3) are under the care of specialist prescribers which we are targeting during the launch of Rezdiffra in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 370,000 patients with MASH with moderate to advanced fibrosis are currently diagnosed and under the care of specialists across Europe.
tamClaims.sentence: In addition, we estimate that approximately 370,000 patients with MASH with moderate to advanced fibrosis are currently diagnosed and under the care of specialists across Europe.
